name: agent-skills
description: Self-contained agent skills for research, industrial R&D, paper writing, PhD applications, language learning, presentation and slides, and coding support. Each skill is standalone with no shared resources between skills.

skills.name: prompting-claude-models
skills.description: Design prompts, system prompts, and agent scaffolding for Claude-backed features; ground volatile model-specific behavior in the live Anthropic docs.

skills.name: industry-problem-framing
skills.description: Convert vague industry problems into researchable, pilotable R&D problem statements.

skills.name: publication-grounded-solution-design
skills.description: Design industry solution directions grounded in prior successful cases, publications, and transfer assumptions.

skills.name: pilot-and-evaluation-design
skills.description: Design practical pilots, baselines, metrics, guardrails, and go/no-go criteria.

skills.name: banking-ai-literature-mapper
skills.description: Map banking AI publications, cases, benchmarks, methods, and evaluation patterns.

skills.name: banking-use-case-discovery
skills.description: Discover and prioritize banking and fintech AI/R&D use cases.

skills.name: research-codebase
skills.description: Structure and maintain a research codebase for fast hypothesis testing.

skills.name: ielts-learning-planner
skills.description: Build a personalised IELTS study plan with weekly schedules, drills, and progress milestones.

skills.name: ielts-reading-strategies
skills.description: Teach, practise, and give feedback on IELTS Academic or General Training Reading strategies.

skills.name: ielts-speaking-coach
skills.description: Simulate, score, and give feedback on IELTS Speaking tests across Parts 1, 2, and 3.

skills.name: ielts-vocabulary-builder
skills.description: Build, drill, and apply IELTS-relevant vocabulary for Writing, Speaking, and Reading.

skills.name: ielts-writing-task1
skills.description: Draft, analyse, score, and improve IELTS Academic Writing Task 1 responses.

skills.name: ielts-writing-task2
skills.description: Draft, analyse, score, and improve IELTS Academic or General Training Writing Task 2 essays.

skills.name: ielts-progress-reporter
skills.description: Aggregate marked IELTS Writing feedback files into periodic progress reports of band trends, recurring errors, and revision gains.

skills.name: ielts-grammar-coach
skills.description: Estimate an IELTS Grammatical Range & Accuracy (GRA) band, then diagnose and durably fix the recurring errors holding it down via coded corrective feedback, Vietnamese-L1 contrastive diagnosis, a persistent error ledger with spaced re-probes, and targeted drills; also a general-English no-band mode. Isolates GRA (use ielts-writing-task2/ielts-speaking-coach for a whole-task band across all four criteria).

skills.name: gre-verbal-reasoning
skills.description: Coach GRE Text Completion and Sentence Equivalence: predict-then-match method, trap taxonomy, practice generation, and miss classification. RC passages go to gre-reading-comprehension; word learning goes to gre-vocabulary-builder.

skills.name: gre-reading-comprehension
skills.description: Coach GRE Reading Comprehension and paragraph-argument (critical reasoning) questions: passage mapping, wrong-answer taxonomy, line-reference discipline, and timed drilling. Fill-in-the-blank items go to gre-verbal-reasoning.

skills.name: gre-quant-coach
skills.description: Coach GRE Quantitative Reasoning: quantitative-comparison tactics, careless-error elimination, content-gap triage, format strategies, and pacing. GRE test math only, not research-level math.

skills.name: gre-issue-essay
skills.description: Draft, score against the 0-6 rubric, and improve GRE Analytical Writing 'Analyze an Issue' essays across all six instruction stems, with a 30-minute timing protocol. IELTS essays go to ielts-writing-task2.

skills.name: gre-vocabulary-builder
skills.description: Build and retain GRE vocabulary via meaning clusters, charge, secondary meanings of common words, and spaced retrieval drills embedded in TC/SE-style contexts. Solving actual TC/SE items goes to gre-verbal-reasoning.

skills.name: gre-learning-planner
skills.description: Build personalised GRE study plans: diagnostic baseline, Verbal/Quant/AW allocation, daily vocabulary pipeline, practice-test milestones, and test-date scheduling around grad-school application deadlines.

skills.name: apply-profile-reader
skills.description: Read academic-career applicant profile files and extract reusable evidence for SOPs, CVs, fit mapping, emails, and application audits.

skills.name: apply-cv-builder
skills.description: Build and audit academic CVs for PhD, research, fellowship, internship, and RA applications.

skills.name: apply-package-auditor
skills.description: Audit a full PhD application package for coherence, missing evidence, risks, and priorities.

skills.name: apply-dossier-evaluator
skills.description: Score a further-education dossier (PhD, master's, scholarship, internship) on a reproducible rubric, with eligibility and scholarship-program fit.

skills.name: apply-program-fit-mapper
skills.description: Map applicant interests to PhD programs, faculty, labs, and school-specific fit evidence.

skills.name: apply-research-direction-mapper
skills.description: Define a professor's recent and trending research direction for fit notes, SOPs, and outreach.

skills.name: apply-cold-email-drafter
skills.description: Draft, critique, and revise concise cold emails to professors and PIs.

skills.name: apply-recommendation-letter-strategist
skills.description: Choose recommenders, plan letter strategy, draft requests, and prepare recommender packets.

skills.name: apply-sop-writer
skills.description: Write, review, score, and iterate Statements of Purpose and PhD application essays.

skills.name: artifact-release-packager
skills.description: Plan and audit research artifact releases for papers.

skills.name: apply-motivation-keeper
skills.description: Help PhD applicants or students recover motivation and rebuild momentum after setbacks.

skills.name: reproducibility-audit
skills.description: Audit whether research results can be reproduced from available code, data, configs, and documentation.

skills.name: experiment-design
skills.description: Design a minimal, decision-relevant experiment with fair baselines and explicit success criteria, and diagnose a training run that has already gone wrong.

skills.name: benchmark-and-baseline-selector
skills.description: Select the minimal and suggested baselines and benchmarks needed to support a research claim.

skills.name: statistical-testing-guide
skills.description: Choose statistical tests, compute effect sizes, plan power, and report statistics correctly.

skills.name: hypothesis-and-ablation-planner
skills.description: Design ablations that isolate causal mechanisms and alternative explanations.

skills.name: model-eval-error-analysis
skills.description: Evaluate models rigorously and inspect where they fail.

skills.name: research-idea-stress-test
skills.description: Stress-test a research idea before major investment.

skills.name: paper-idea-and-scope-brainstormer
skills.description: Brainstorm a research idea and scope what a new paper should cover, upstream of stress-testing and argument planning.

skills.name: cross-domain-analogy-finder
skills.description: Find a concept's structural twin in a distant field to borrow a portable solution (offense) or check whether an idea already exists (defense), with a four-way verification gate and prior-art grounding.

skills.name: gap-motivation-builder
skills.description: Socratically build the motivation case for filling a research gap — consequence, beneficiary, blocker, timeliness, payoff — ending in a motivation verdict plus solution desiderata, without proposing the method.

skills.name: pytorch-training-recipe
skills.description: Produce an implementable PyTorch or Lightning training recipe.

skills.name: jax-training-recipe
skills.description: Produce an implementable JAX, Flax, and Optax training recipe.

skills.name: paper-to-code
skills.description: Reproduce a paper's method in minimal, direct PyTorch when no official code is available; staged verification gates and an assumptions ledger decide whether the method works as claimed.

skills.name: related-work-writer
skills.description: Write or revise a Related Work section from a source-grounded literature map.

skills.name: survey-paper-writer
skills.description: Plan, draft, and revise standalone survey, tutorial, review, and perspective papers whose main contribution is synthesis.

skills.name: citation-auditor
skills.description: Audit citations for existence, attribution, and claim-to-source accuracy.

skills.name: literature-triangulation
skills.description: Build a source-grounded map of a research topic by comparing papers, benchmarks, code, and disagreements.

skills.name: abstract-and-intro-writer
skills.description: Draft or revise the abstract and introduction of a research paper.

skills.name: method-section-writer
skills.description: Draft or revise the method section of a research paper with consistent notation and motivation.

skills.name: discussion-report-synthesizer
skills.description: Synthesize fragmented academic conversations into cohesive, structured markdown reports.

skills.name: figure-table-planner
skills.description: Plan and audit figures, tables, captions, and visual evidence for a research paper.

skills.name: paper-argument-planner
skills.description: Plan the argument of a research paper from ideas, drafts, results, or notes.

skills.name: results-writeup
skills.description: Turn verified research findings into calibrated scientific writing.

skills.name: research-proposal-writer
skills.description: Write or refine a forward-looking research proposal — a multi-year PhD research plan for a prospective supervisor or fellowship, or a next-paper pitch to your advisor.

skills.name: research-talk-planner
skills.description: Plan and structure a research talk from a paper, results, or research agenda.

skills.name: reviewer-response-strategist
skills.description: Plan rebuttals, reviewer responses, and revision strategies.

skills.name: peer-review-writer
skills.description: Write a peer review of someone else's paper for a venue: structured summary, evidenced strengths and weaknesses, calibrated scores, and constructive questions; also covers serving on a program committee or as area chair (bidding, COI, subreviewer management, discussion).

skills.name: submission-readiness-audit
skills.description: Audit a paper, appendix, or submission package before deadline.

skills.name: venue-targeting
skills.description: Select and prioritize publication venues and plan resubmission strategy after rejection.

skills.name: professor-mentor-technical-teaching
skills.description: Explain advanced technical topics with professor-style intuition, rigor, implementation grounding, and critical judgment.

skills.name: concept-exercise-generator
skills.description: Generate a graded easy-to-advanced exercise set with separated solutions to verify understanding of a topic, method, derivation, or paper; multiple-choice items use misconception-coded distractors and intuitive-trap items force deliberate verification.

skills.name: knowledge-debt-audit
skills.description: Detect AI-produced results the user cannot regenerate unaided, probe the load-bearing step before they build on it, and schedule variable-cue re-probes after repayment.

skills.name: professor-critic
skills.description: Critique a finished artifact the way its specific named reader would, returning a verdict-first strict-professor teardown with a FATAL/MAJOR/MINOR severity ladder rather than encouragement. Requires a named reader and acceptance bar and refuses to grade blind or to judge mid-draft work. Distinct from peer-review-writer (user reviews someone else's paper), submission-readiness-audit (paper-only author checklist), and knowledge-debt-audit (probes the user's understanding, not the artifact).

skills.name: naive-student
skills.description: Become an honest novice while the user teaches a concept, asking only earned questions and ending with a fallible playback that localizes missing dependencies.

skills.name: whiteboard-peer
skills.description: Co-solve unfinished research ideas with committed partial attempts and productive disagreement while refusing to provide the complete solution.

skills.name: gap-finder
skills.description: Surface publishable theoretical gaps in research papers and run weekly lemma autopsies by weakening one hypothesis before consulting the authors' discussion.

skills.name: argument-audit
skills.description: Interactively and Socratically check whether stated evidence or premises establish a claim in a proof, essay, research conclusion, or everyday argument; converges to a validity verdict plus the smallest fix or counterexample.

skills.name: theorem-and-claim-audit
skills.description: Pressure-test mathematical arguments, step-by-step derivations, and ML paper claims, and deliver a report-style audit.

skills.name: theory-assumption-extractor
skills.description: Extract explicit and hidden assumptions from proofs, models, and empirical arguments.

skills.name: theory-counterexample-hunter
skills.description: Search for edge cases and constructions that break claims or weaken theorems.

skills.name: theory-formalism-translator
skills.description: Translate ideas across optimization, probability, information theory, dynamical systems, graph theory, and statistics.

skills.name: theory-paper-to-theorem-distiller
skills.description: Distill dense theory papers into definitions, claims, proof strategy, and reusable techniques.

skills.name: theory-proof-sketcher
skills.description: Convert informal mathematical ideas into theorem, lemma, or proof scaffolds.

skills.name: theory-to-toy-cases
skills.description: Turn abstract math or ML ideas into minimal examples, counterexamples, sanity checks, and simulations.

skills.name: flow-learn-new-topic
skills.description: Orchestrate breadth-first onboarding into an unfamiliar research area by sequencing singleton research skills.

skills.name: flow-deep-understanding
skills.description: Orchestrate depth-first mastery of one paper, method, or theorem by sequencing singleton research skills.

skills.name: flow-idea-to-proof
skills.description: Orchestrate a raw idea into a formal claim and defensible proof design by sequencing singleton research skills.

skills.name: flow-paper-lifecycle
skills.description: Orchestrate a research paper end-to-end from idea to artifact release, owning the phase-gated writing stage-group and delegating every other stage.

skills.name: flow-phd-application
skills.description: Orchestrate the PhD application process from profile to CV, fit, SOP, recommenders, outreach, and package audit.

skills.name: deck-beamer-academic
skills.description: Build compile-ready LaTeX Beamer decks for academic talks, seminars, conferences, defenses, and lectures.

skills.name: deck-beamer-proposal-report
skills.description: Build compile-ready LaTeX Beamer decks for research proposals, progress reports, milestone reviews, and final reports.

skills.name: deck-business-report
skills.description: Build PowerPoint or Canva business report decks for executives, managers, clients, pilots, and stakeholder updates.

skills.name: init-workspace
skills.description: Initialize a non-coding, note-centric Obsidian workspace for learning, ongoing research notes, research writing, academic applications, talks, test preparation, theory, or similar knowledge work. One short intake batch covers the work and definition of done, title plus confirmed target directory, and differences from the typical case. The skill then uses one of seven ready templates as-is (including research-notebook), adapts the closest template while keeping its Layout and Operating contract consistent, or writes a lean bespoke vault when none fits. Every result includes a folder tree, an operating-manual AGENTS.md, and a CLAUDE.md import; references and a Dataview dashboard are conditional on the template or intake. Full-pipeline templates, including research-notebook, and substantial bespoke research/synthesis vaults receive the shared one-file evidence-draft-review-verify protocol. Seeded prose uses one physical line per paragraph or list item and relies on Obsidian soft wrapping. The skill never scaffolds software/code projects, modifies repository templates during a run, or overwrites existing vault files.
